isolation.notes: All validation commands used nix develop -c ... and DUNE_BUILD_DIR under the mission evidence directory so the shared _build/ tree was not touched.

toolsUsed: nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR="/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round3/dune-build" dune exec rgoc -- "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round3/fixtures/stdlib_cross_bridge.rg" -o "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round3/outputs/stdlib_cross_bridge.go"

assertions.title: Stdlib call sites use snake_case while stdlib type names stay PascalCase

steps.action: Compile one representative fixture that imports net::http and uses http::listen_and_serve, http::new_serve_mux, mux.handle_func, http::Request, http::ResponseWriter, and http::ServeMux.
steps.expected: rgoc exits 0 and writes the requested generated Go file.
steps.observed: rgoc exited 0. The requested -o path did not exist before the compile and did exist afterward (`pre_compile_output_exists=false`, `post_compile_output_exists=true`). The compile log showed only the nix shell banner and parser-generator warnings, with no rgoc semantic diagnostic.

steps.action: Run downstream Go acceptance checks on the generated file.
steps.expected: gofmt -d is clean, go build succeeds, and go vet succeeds.
steps.observed: gofmt -d exited 0 and emitted no diff hunks. The first go build/go vet attempt from the evidence directory failed because `nix develop` could not locate `flake.nix`; rerunning both from the repo root exited 0/0 and produced `outputs/stdlib_cross_bridge.bin`.

steps.action: Inspect the generated Go for the lowered stdlib spellings.
steps.observed: Generated Go contained `import "net/http"`, `func handle_request(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)`, `mux := http.NewServeMux()`, `mux.HandleFunc("/ok", handle_request)`, and `http.ListenAndServe(":8080", mux)`.

commands: nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR="/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round3/dune-build" dune exec rgoc -- "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round3/fixtures/stdlib_cross_bridge.rg" -o "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round3/outputs/stdlib_cross_bridge.go"
diagnostics: initial go build exit code from evidence dir: 1 (`error: could not find a flake.nix file`)
initial go vet exit code from evidence dir: 1 (`error: could not find a flake.nix file`)
retry go build exit code from repo root: 0
retry go vet exit code from repo root: 0

assertions.title: Import, naming, and stdlib type usage compose coherently in one program

steps.action: Use the same single fixture to combine `use net::http`, snake_case stdlib call names, and PascalCase stdlib types in one program.
steps.expected: The combined import+naming+type flow compiles cleanly to generated Go.
steps.observed: The combined fixture compiled successfully with `compile_exit_code=0`, and the requested generated-Go file existed after the command.

steps.action: Validate the emitted Go with downstream Go tools.
steps.expected: Formatter, build, and vet checks succeed for the same combined program.
steps.observed: gofmt -d exited 0. After rerunning from the repo root so `nix develop` could resolve the flake, go build and go vet both exited 0 for the generated file.

steps.action: Inspect the emitted Go to confirm the combined lowering of import namespace, callable names, and type positions.
steps.expected: Generated Go contains `import "net/http"` and the lowered function/type spellings together in one file.
steps.observed: The same generated file contained `import "net/http"`, `http.NewServeMux`, `mux.HandleFunc`, `http.ListenAndServe`, `http.ResponseWriter`, and `*http.Request` together.

commands: nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR="/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round3/dune-build" dune exec rgoc -- "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round3/fixtures/stdlib_cross_bridge.rg" -o "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round3/outputs/stdlib_cross_bridge.go"

frictions.description: Running `nix develop` from the evidence `outputs/` directory failed because that directory is outside the repository and therefore outside the flake lookup path.
frictions.resolution: Reran `go build` and `go vet` from `/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang` while keeping outputs under the assigned evidence directory.

frictions.description: The compile and Go-tool logs include the flake shell banner, and the rgoc compile log also includes parser-generator warnings before the actual validation result.
frictions.resolution: Captured explicit exit-code/status files plus a terminal snapshot and generated-Go reference log so the real outcomes remained easy to verify.

summary: Retested 2 import-surface assertions. Both now pass: the representative fixture compiled with rgoc (exit 0), emitted generated Go containing `import "net/http"`, `http.ListenAndServe`, `http.NewServeMux`, `mux.HandleFunc`, `http.ResponseWriter`, and `*http.Request`, and the generated file passed `gofmt -d`, `go build`, and `go vet` after rerunning the Go commands from the repo root to satisfy flake lookup.
